fields.label: What is your first name?
fields.helpText: Your first name and middle initial. Example: Joe A

fields.label: What is your last name?
What is your Social Security Number?
fields.helpText: Your 9-digit SSN. This stays private — your employer needs it for tax reporting.

fields.label: What is your home address?
fields.helpText: Street address, apartment, or suite number.

fields.label: City, state, and ZIP code?
How do you file your taxes?
fields.helpText: Not sure? Most single people pick the first option. Married couples usually pick the second.

options.label: Head of household (single parent or sole provider)

fields.label: Do you have more than one job, or does your spouse also work?
fields.helpText: If you and/or your spouse work multiple jobs at the same time, your taxes need to be calculated differently so too little isn't withheld.

options.label: Yes, exactly 2 jobs total between us

fields.label: Check here if there are only 2 jobs total (most accurate withholding)
fields.helpText: The IRS recommends checking this box if you and your spouse have exactly 2 jobs total. Do the same on the W-4 for the other job.

fields.label: Do you want to use the Multiple Jobs Worksheet for more accurate withholding?
fields.helpText: The worksheet gives a more precise result. If you don't want to fill it out, you can skip it — the checkbox above is a simpler option.

fields.label: Worksheet Line 1: Two highest-paying jobs annual wages
fields.helpText: Using the tables on page 3 of the W-4, find the value at the intersection of the two highest-paying jobs.

fields.label: Worksheet Line 2a: First job annual wages
fields.helpText: Enter the annual wages for your highest-paying job.

fields.label: Worksheet Line 2b: Second job annual wages
fields.helpText: Enter the annual wages for your second highest-paying job.

fields.label: Worksheet Line 2c: Third job annual wages (if applicable)
fields.helpText: If you have a third job, enter its annual wages. Otherwise leave blank.

fields.label: Worksheet Line 3: Number of pay periods
fields.helpText: How many pay periods per year at the highest-paying job? Weekly=52, Bi-weekly=26, Semi-monthly=24, Monthly=12.

fields.label: Worksheet Line 4: Extra withholding per paycheck
fields.helpText: Divide Line 1 by Line 3. This is the extra amount to withhold each paycheck. Enter this in Step 4c.

fields.label: Will your total income be $200,000 or less ($400,000 or less if married filing jointly)?
fields.helpText: Step 3 uses these income thresholds for the dependent credits.

fields.label: How many kids under 17 do you have?
fields.helpText: Children you support who are under 17. For 2026, multiply this count by $2,200. Put 0 if none.

fields.label: Do you support anyone else? (elderly parents, adult children, etc.)
fields.helpText: Other people who depend on you financially. Each one gets a $500 credit. Put 0 if none.

fields.label: Do you have other income besides your job? How much per year?
fields.helpText: Money from interest, dividends, rental property, side gigs, retirement, etc. Enter the yearly amount. Put 0 or leave blank if none.

fields.label: Do you need to use the 2026 Deductions Worksheet?
fields.helpText: Use it for qualified tips/overtime/vehicle-loan interest, the senior deduction, adjustments, itemized deductions, or qualifying cash gifts. The 2026 standard deductions are $16,100 single/MFS, $32,200 MFJ/QSS, and $24,150 head of household.

options.label: No, I'll take the standard deduction (most people)
Yes, I want to fill out the Deductions Worksheet

fields.label: Step 4(b): Deductions amount
fields.helpText: If you used the official 2026 Deductions Worksheet, enter Line 15 here. Otherwise enter 0.

fields.label: Want extra tax taken out of each paycheck?
fields.helpText: Some people like to have extra money withheld so they get a bigger refund. Enter a dollar amount per paycheck, or 0.

fields.label: I'm exempt from taxes
fields.helpText: Only check this if: (1) you got back ALL your taxes last year, AND (2) you expect the same this year. Most people do NOT check this.

fields.label: Employee signature
fields.helpText: Form W-4 is not valid unless the employee signs it.

fields.label: Date signed
Employer's name and address (if you know it)
fields.placeholder: Acme Corp, 100 Industry Blvd, Dallas TX 75201
fields.helpText: Your employer usually fills this in, but you can enter it if you want.

fields.label: First date of employment (if applicable)
fields.helpText: The date you started or will start this job. Your employer may fill this in.

fields.label: Employer's EIN (if you know it)
fields.helpText: Employer Identification Number. Your employer usually fills this in.
